title: B7-33
description: B7-33 is a synthetic, single-chain peptidomimetic agonist of the relaxin family peptide receptor 1 (RXFP1), derived from the B-chain of human gene-2 relaxin. It is designed to replicate the vasoprotective and anti-fibrotic effects of native relaxin while being more cost-effective to produce and possessing improved pharmacological properties.

research.title: B7-33, a Functionally Selective Relaxin Receptor 1 Agonist, Attenuates Myocardial Infarction-Related Adverse Cardiac Remodeling in Mice
research.summary: This study found that B7-33 reduces heart damage and scarring after a heart attack by activating specific protective pathways without the side effects of traditional relaxin treatments.

research.title: B7-33 replicates the vasoprotective functions of human gene-2 relaxin
research.summary: Research demonstrates that B7-33 effectively mimics the vascular benefits of human relaxin, promoting blood vessel health and reducing systemic vascular resistance.

sentiment.summary: User interest is high due to its anti-fibrotic potential and cardiovascular benefits, though it is primarily discussed in specialized peptide and biohacking circles rather than general supplement forums.

effects.label: Cardiac Health:
effects.description: Highly effective in animal models for preventing adverse remodeling after myocardial infarction.

effects.label: Vascular Protection:
effects.description: Successfully replicates the beneficial effects of serelaxin on endothelial function.

effects.label: Targeted Activation:
effects.description: Shows functional selectivity for RXFP1, minimizing off-target signaling pathways.

dosage.label: Research Dosing:
dosage.description: Animal studies often utilize weight-based dosing; human anecdotal ranges typically fall between 1-5mg daily.

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Due to its peptide nature, it is typically administered via subcutaneous injection daily or every other day.

dosage.label: Loading Phase:
dosage.description: Some users suggest a short high-dose period to achieve steady state, though this is not clinically validated.

sideEffects.label: Blood Pressure Drops:
sideEffects.description: Potential for hypotension due to its potent vasodilatory properties.

sideEffects.label: Injection Site Reaction:
sideEffects.description: Possible redness, itching, or swelling at the site of administration.

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia:
sideEffects.description: Minimal reports suggest potential interactions with glucose metabolism in sensitive users.

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Currently sold as a 'research chemical' and not approved by the FDA for human consumption.

availability.label: Sourcing:
availability.description: Primarily available through specialized peptide supply companies and compounding pharmacies.
